meta.title: ECR-XVII Sporley's Inventory of the Regalia English coronation record pilot
meta.note: ECR includes ordines, narrative records, papal letters, inventories, processions, and printed orders; reference anchors are not asserted physical manuscripts.

ordines.label: Sporley's Inventory of the Regalia
ordines.description: The inventory of the regalia, compiled by Sporley, a monk of Westminster {fl. circa 1450) is found in a list of relics at Westminster Abbey, made in the middle of the fifteenth century. All the sacerdotal vestments are mentioned, and the royal ornaments, with the exception of the ring. The golden sceptre is doubtless the sceptre with the cross, the wooden rod, the rod with the dove, and the iron rod may possibly be St. Edward's staff. The comb of St. Edward is here mentioned, as also a spoon for unction. The chalice of St. Edward is said to be of onyx stone, with a foot, rivets, and paten of the best gold. W.= Westminster: Chapter Library, MS. Catalogue, p. 32, lohannis Fkete monachi Westmonasteriensis capitulum xiiii. no foliation, a contemporary copy of the inventory.
